activities_projects.title: Heartbeat and Pulse Observation
activities_projects.objective: Observe and count the pulse rate
steps: Place your index and middle fingers on the inner side of your left wrist
Feel the throbbing (pulse) movements
Count the number of pulse beats in one minute while resting
Run for 2 minutes and count the pulse rate again
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the relationship between physical activity and heart rate
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct counting and comparison of resting vs. active pulse (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Stethoscope Model
activities_projects.objective: Build a simple device to hear the heartbeat
steps: Fix the funnel on one end of the rubber tube
Stretch a rubber sheet over the mouth of the funnel and fix it with a rubber band
Put the open end of the tube in your ear and the funnel on your chest
Listen to the 'lub-dub' sound of your heart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns how sound can be amplified to listen to internal organs
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful construction and hearing of heartbeat (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Breathing Rate Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the average breathing rate of a person
steps: Sit comfortably and breathe normally
Count one breath as one 'inhalation + exhalation'
Count how many breaths you take in one minute
Compare your result with a family member
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines breathing rate and observes individual variation
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate recording of breaths per minute (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Exhaled Air and Lime Water
activities_projects.objective: Prove that we exhale carbon dioxide
steps: Pour some lime water into the test tube
Gently blow air into the lime water using the straw
Observe the change in the color of the lime water
Note if it turns milky
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Confirms the presence of carbon dioxide in exhaled air
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct observation of the lime water turning milky (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Model of the Human Lungs
steps: Cut the bottom of the bottle and fix the Y-tube through the cap
Tie balloons to the ends of the Y-tube (acting as lungs)
Tie a rubber sheet at the bottom (acting as diaphragm)
Pull the rubber sheet down and observe the balloons inflate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes how the diaphragm helps in lung expansion
activities_projects.evaluation: Working model showing inflation and deflation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Human Excretory System Diagram
activities_projects.objective: Identify the organs of the urinary system
steps: Draw the two kidney-shaped organs
Draw the ureters, urinary bladder, and urethra
Label each part clearly
Color the kidneys dark red
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the pathway of urine removal from the body
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling and neatness of the diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Blood Components Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the different parts of blood
steps: Divide the chart into four sections: RBC, WBC, Platelets, and Plasma
Write one function for each (e.g., RBC carries oxygen)
Explain why blood is red in color
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Knows the functions of various blood cells and plasma
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct description of all four components (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chest Expansion Measurement
activities_projects.objective: Measure the change in chest size during breathing
steps: Measure the chest size after deep inhalation
Measure the chest size after complete exhalation
Calculate the difference
Discuss why the size increases
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Observes the physical change in the thoracic cavity during respiration
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct measurement and calculation of the difference (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Path of Food
activities_projects.objective: Trace the journey of food in the digestive system
steps: List the organs from mouth to anus in order
Write what happens to food in the stomach briefly
Mention where the final digestion takes place
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the sequence of the human digestive tract
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow and correct order of organs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sweat and Cooling
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of sweat in temperature regulation
steps: Apply some water to the back of your hand using the cotton ball
Stand under a fan or wave your hand
Notice the cooling sensation
Relate this to how sweating helps our body
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains evaporation as a cooling process for the body
activities_projects.evaluation: Personal observation and explanation of the cooling effect (10 marks)
